reasoning: EOG declared a dividend of $0.975 per share with an ex-dividend date on April 16, 2025. The base price is locked in prior to this, on Friday, April 11. By the time of the first resolution date on April 20 (measured by April 17 close), the stock price will have structurally dropped by roughly 0.75%. Over a brief 4-trading-day window, natural positive market drift will likely be insufficient to overcome this deterministic drop, leading to a <50% probability (43%) for the initial date. Moving to the second date in mid-May, the price has roughly 20 trading days for the positive drift to help recover the dividend drop, bringing the probability closer to even (48%). For July and October, despite a likely second dividend ex-date in July, standard 8-10% annualized stock market drift gives the edge to a higher price over the original baseline, pushing the estimates slightly above 50% (51% and 52% respectively). In line with increasing systemic uncertainty over longer time horizons, the distant date estimates remain anchored strongly toward 0.50.
